name: Haggis#3
recipeIngredient: The liver, heart, and lungs of the sheep
2 - 3 cups broth (in which the liver, heart and lungs were cooked)
recipeInstructions:
What you need:
Canning kettle or a large spaghetti pot, 16- to 20 quart size with a lid to fit it
Meat grinder
Cheesecloth
1. If the butcher has not already cut apart and trimmed the heart, liver and lungs, do that first.
- Cut the lungs off the windpipe
- Cut the heart off the large blood vessels
- Cut it open to rinse it for quicker cooking
- The liver must be freed from the rest
2. Put them in a 4-quart pot with 2 to 3 cups water, bring to boil and simmer for about an hour and a half.
- Let it all cool and keep the broth
- Run the liver and heart through the meat grinder
- Cut out as much of the gristly part as easily possible, then run lungs through the grinder too
- Put raw beef suet through the grinder
- As you finish grinding each thing, add it to the big kettle
- Peel, slice and chop onions, then add them to the meat in the kettle
- Add salt and spices and mix
3. Put oatmeal into the kettle
- Toast or lightly brown in oven or heavy-bottomed pan is optional
- When thoroughly mixed with rest of ingredients, add 2 cups of broth left from boiling meat
- If handful sticks together when taken, do not add third cup of broth
- If crumbly and does not hold together well, add the rest of broth and mix thoroughly
4. Stuff stomach (smooth side out) about three-quarters full with mixture
- Sew up openings
- Wrap in cheesecloth for easy handling during cooking
5. Wash kettle and bring 2 gallons water to boil
- Put haggis in and prick all over with skewer
- Boil gently for about 4 or 5 hours
- If no cheesecloth, use large clean dish towel; work under kitchen spoons to form a sling
- Wear lined rubber gloves when lifting out haggis
- Note: Rinse stomach shaggy side in cold water several times until clear and sediment minimal
